---
title: "ggsql + dbt"
subtitle: "posit hackathon 2026"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: false
    theme: default
    highlight-style: github
    css: overview.css
---

<div class="exec-summary">

This is an early exploration of what it might look like to integrate [ggsql](https://github.com/posit-dev/ggsql) with dbt. ggsql extends SQL with Grammar of Graphics syntax, so a query can describe a visualization alongside the data it needs. The question this project asks is whether that fits naturally into a dbt pipeline — and if so, how.

Three prototypes are here for discussion. A **Makefile demo** chains `dbt build` and the ggsql CLI with shell scripts — simple enough to show the idea quickly. A **community extension** wraps that into a pip-installable package. A **dbt-core fork** adds `visualization` as a native node type in the dbt graph. None of these are production-ready; they're rough starting points for a conversation.

</div>

---

## The idea

dbt engineers spend most of their time in SQL — building staging views, intermediate models, and mart tables. Visualization is typically a separate step, in a separate tool. ggsql suggests a different approach: SQL with visualization syntax built in. The same query can describe both the data it needs and how to display it.

```sql
SELECT team, pts, reb FROM mart_player_stats
VISUALISE pts AS x, reb AS y, team AS color
DRAW point
LABEL title => 'Scoring vs. Rebounding',
      x => 'Points', y => 'Rebounds'
```

Whether this belongs in a dbt workflow is an open question. These prototypes are an attempt to work through what the ergonomics might look like.

## Try it: the Makefile demo

The simplest entry point is a Makefile that chains `dbt build` and the ggsql CLI together. It's rough but transparent, and it makes the concept concrete quickly.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo
cd dbtggsql-demo
make report
```

The demo uses a fictional 2026 NBA Finals dataset (Knicks vs. Spurs) running in DuckDB. Three output modes are wired up:

| command | output |
|---|---|
| `OUTPUT=cli make` | individual chart files |
| `OUTPUT=static make` | a static navigable bundle |
| `make` | a Quarto narrative report |

---

## Approach 1 — community extension

This wraps the Makefile idea into a pip package with a proper CLI:

```bash
pip install dbt-ggsql
dbt-ggsql build                  # dbt build + render → HTML
dbt-ggsql build --output qmd     # → Quarto document with code-foldable SQL
dbt-ggsql build --output both    # → both
```

`.ggsql` files sit in a `visualizations/` folder alongside the dbt project. The extension runs them after `dbt build` and assembles the output into a static HTML file or a Quarto document. It doesn't require changes to dbt-core.

This approach is the most self-contained of the three. It still has rough edges.

→ [github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-extension-pkg](https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-extension-pkg)

---

## Approach 2 — dbt-core visualization node

This fork of dbt-core adds `visualization` as a node type. `.ggsql` files appear in `dbt ls`, can be targeted with `--select`, and run as part of `dbt build` alongside models and seeds.

```bash
dbt build                              # runs models and visualizations
dbt run --select shot_chart            # target one visualization
dbt ls --resource-type visualization   # list visualization nodes
```

The fork is minimal and not kept in sync with upstream — it's more a sketch of what native integration might look like than something you'd actually maintain. But it demonstrates the idea.

→ [github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-modifieddbt](https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-modifieddbt)

---

## Sample output

Rendered examples from each approach — self-contained HTML, no installation needed.

<div class="sample-grid">

<div class="sample-card">
<div class="sample-thumb">
<img src="how-it-works.svg" alt="how it works diagram" />
</div>
<div class="sample-info">
<div class="sample-label">Makefile demo · full report</div>
<h3><a href="https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo/blob/main/docs/sample_nba_report.html" target="_blank">NBA Report →</a></h3>
<p>Shot charts, game margins, efficiency breakdowns, and player scatter plots, rendered via the Quarto report pipeline from ggsql queries over dbt mart tables.</p>
<div class="sample-tags">
<span class="tag">Quarto</span>
<span class="tag">11 charts</span>
<span class="tag">DuckDB</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="sample-card">
<div class="sample-thumb sample-thumb-dark">
<img src="../../dbtggsql-demo-modifieddbt/docs/native-node.svg" alt="native node diagram" />
</div>
<div class="sample-info">
<div class="sample-label">dbt-core fork · native node</div>
<h3><a href="https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-modifieddbt/blob/main/docs/sample_shot_chart.html" target="_blank">Shot Chart →</a></h3>
<p>A single visualization node running as a dbt node type. The shot chart renders inside <code>dbt build</code> alongside the models it depends on.</p>
<div class="sample-tags">
<span class="tag">dbt-core fork</span>
<span class="tag">NodeType.Visualization</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="sample-card">
<div class="sample-thumb sample-thumb-teal">
<div class="sample-thumb-label">
<div class="thumb-icon">⬡</div>
<div class="thumb-text">HTML output</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="sample-info">
<div class="sample-label">Community extension · HTML</div>
<h3><a href="https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-extension-pkg/blob/main/docs/sample_visualizations.html" target="_blank">visualizations.html →</a></h3>
<p>Static SVG grid from <code>dbt-ggsql build --output html</code>. Shot charts, efficiency comparisons, and player scatter plots.</p>
<div class="sample-tags">
<span class="tag">--output html</span>
<span class="tag">inline SVG</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="sample-card">
<div class="sample-thumb sample-thumb-navy">
<div class="sample-thumb-label">
<div class="thumb-icon">▶</div>
<div class="thumb-text">Quarto output</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="sample-info">
<div class="sample-label">Community extension · Quarto</div>
<h3><a href="https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/icarusz/dbtggsql-demo-extension-pkg/blob/main/docs/sample_visualizations_qmd.html" target="_blank">visualizations_qmd.html →</a></h3>
<p>Quarto output from <code>dbt-ggsql build --output qmd</code>. Each chart in its own section; SQL visible via <code>▶ show query</code>.</p>
<div class="sample-tags">
<span class="tag">--output qmd</span>
<span class="tag">code-fold SQL</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>

</div>
